western.definition: Ability to impose will regardless of conditions; resistance to change or deformation; dominance over opposing forces.

relational.definition: Adaptive capacity of body/mind/environment system; efficient energy usage and transmission; structural resilience under varied conditions.

hidden_variables.name: Energy flow efficiency
hidden_variables.description: How well energy moves through the system without dissipation.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Force-based metrics measure peak load, not throughput economy.

hidden_variables.name: Structural organization
hidden_variables.description: How distributed and redundant the load-bearing topology is.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Single-axis strength tests reduce structure to a scalar.

hidden_variables.name: Multi-system integration
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Component-level testing isolates the part from its support network.

translation_protocol.when_to_translate: When 'strength' is being measured by single-axis force resistance and adaptive resilience is being ignored.
translation_protocol.how: Ask: 'Strength against what perturbation, on what timescale, at what cost to the rest of the system?'
